pattern: Instead of actually mutating every remaining battery on each test, track how many tests have already happened -- that running count is exactly how much every later device's percentage has effectively dropped.
hints: Simulating the drop on every device to the right after each test works, but notice that a device further along only ever loses one percentage point per test that happened before it, never per device that happened before it and skipped testing.
Keep a single running total of how many devices have been tested so far and treat that as the amount subtracted from the current device's original percentage before deciding whether it passes.

Walk the array once: for each device, compare its original percentage minus the running tested-count to zero; if that difference is positive, count the device as tested and increase the running count by one, otherwise leave the count unchanged and move on.

edge_inputs.description: mixed percentages with some zeros appearing after decrements
leading zero skips the first device
single device at the maximum percentage
all ones: only the first device is ever tested since it drains the rest to zero
strictly decreasing percentages that shrink faster once decrements stack up
one hundred devices all at the maximum percentage

stress.description: random battery percentages across the full allowed length
stress.note: uniform random percentages exercise many different decrement/skip patterns
